name: Thai Chicken Salad
recipeIngredient: 20 Won ton skins - cut into 1/4 inch strips
4 cups Barbecued or roast chicken - in bite-size pieces (from a 3 lb bird)
1 large Yellow bell pepper - cut into thin julienne
1/2 European seedless cucumber - cut into thin julienne
1 tablespoon Fresh lemon grass (optional) - finely minced
1/4 cup Dry-roasted unsalted peanuts - coarsely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large skillet, heat the oil over moderately high heat until a strip of won ton bounces across the surface. Add the won ton strips in batches and fry, turning until crisp and golden, about 1 minute. Transfer to paper towels; drain well.
2. In a large bowl, combine the mixed greens, chicken, bean sprouts, yellow pepper, and cucumber.
3. In a medium bowl, whisk together the lime juice, fish sauce, brown sugar, chiles, nutmeg, lemon grass, ginger, mint, and basil. Add the dressing to the salad and toss well. Gently fold in the crisp won ton strips.
4. Turn out onto a serving platter and sprinkle with the peanuts.
